channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Jannik Sinner Secures Second Wimbledon Title After Four-Set Victory Over Zverev
item.description: Jannik Sinner, the world number one, successfully defended his Wimbledon title with a hard-fought four-set victory over French Open champion Alexander Zverev. The match, which lasted three hours and 4...

item.title: 14 Countries Reaffirm 2016 Arbitral Ruling Against China's South China Sea Claims
item.description: Fourteen countries, including the Philippines, reaffirmed in a joint statement that the 2016 arbitral ruling against China's South China Sea claims remains valid. The statement, released by the Philip...

item.title: Former South Carolina Senator Lindsey Graham Dies After Sudden Illness
item.description: Lindsey Graham, a longtime Republican senator from South Carolina, passed away on Saturday night following a brief illness, according to his office. The news has sparked tributes from President Donald...

item.title: One Nation's poll decline and Thai pub tragedy dominate headlines
item.description: The article details multiple key events across Australia and internationally. Politically, One Nation's support has dropped for the first time in four months, shifting to the Coalition as women and im...

item.title: Trump administration subpoenas journalists over Air Force One story, experts call it troubling
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump's administration has subpoenaed two New York Times reporters for a story revealing that he used a Qatari-funded Air Force One to travel to the UK instead of the regular Air...

item.title: India Observes National Mourning for Late Qatar Amir Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al-Thani
item.description: The Indian government has announced a day of national mourning on July 13 to honor Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al-Thani, the late Father Amir of Qatar, who passed away at 74. The national flag will be fl...

item.title: U.S. Senator Lindsey Graham, Trump Ally, Dies at 71 After Brief Illness
item.description: Prominent U.S. Senator Lindsey Graham, a key ally of former President Donald Trump, died on Saturday aged 71 following a 'brief and sudden illness,' his office confirmed. Graham, known for his hawkish...

item.title: SANDF Funding and Accountability: Addressing Waste Before Seeking More Resources
item.description: The article critiques the South African National Defence Force's (SANDF) funding challenges, arguing that accountability for existing resources must precede demands for additional funding. Democratic ...

item.title: Late Bloomers: Secrets to Starting New Careers in Later Life
item.description: The article explores the journeys of individuals who have embarked on second careers later in life, offering insights into their strategies for success. Fiona Leitch, a novelist who began writing in h...

item.title: Wildfire Forces Evacuations in East Kootenay as Firefighters Battle Flames Near Boston Bar
item.description: A wildfire in British Columbia's southeast has prompted an evacuation alert for 72 properties near Premier Lake, with the Lussier River fire burning approximately 1.15 square kilometers. The Regional ...

item.title: GetUp's strategic shift in combating One Nation's political rise
item.description: The article explores GetUp's evolving strategy to counter the growing influence of One Nation, led by Pauline Hanson. Following a controversial protest at the National Press Club where a banner critic...

item.title: WWE Champion Rhea Ripley discusses meniscus injury and recovery progress
item.description: Rhea Ripley, the WWE Women's Champion, has been sidelined due to a slight meniscus tear in her knee. During a recent appearance at a Power Slap event, Ripley provided an update on her health, explaini...

item.title: Canadian Group Promotes National Outdoors Time for Unity
item.description: A newly formed initiative called Everyone Outdoors Together aims to unite Canadians by encouraging them to spend time in nature on July 18th, coinciding with National Parks Day. Led by Martin Sampson ...

item.title: Small towns lead in solar adoption, outpacing big cities
item.description: The article highlights how smaller urban centers like Lucknow, Nagpur, and Surat are leading India's energy transition by adopting rooftop solar systems more than major cities. These districts, along ...

item.title: Lindsey Graham's Death Sparks Political Controversy Amid Speculation About Foreign Involvement
item.description: The sudden death of U.S. Senator Lindsey Graham has triggered widespread speculation and political turmoil in Washington. Reports suggest his passing may be linked to alleged foreign interference, wit...

item.title: Tragic fire at Bangkok pub kills at least 27
item.description: A devastating fire broke out at a pub in Bangkok, Thailand, early on Monday morning, resulting in the deaths of at least 27 people. The incident, which occurred at the Na Ladprao pub (also known as Ro...

item.title: Texas Hispanics Shift Support to Talarico Amid Immigration Policy Concerns
item.description: A recent survey of Hispanic business owners in Texas reveals a significant shift in political support, with many moving away from Republican candidates due to concerns over Trump's immigration policie...

item.title: Iran and the US escalate conflict over control of critical energy corridor in the Gulf
item.description: Iran has intensified its military operations in the Gulf, launching missile strikes against U.S. facilities in multiple countries including Qatar and the UAE, while declaring it has closed the vital S...

item.title: England Test coach Brendon McCullum resigns after string of losses and off-field controversies
item.description: Brendon McCullum has been removed as England's Test head coach following a series of poor results and off-field issues. The decision comes after England's Test team lost seven of their last nine match...

item.title: Analyzing the Greens' Decline and Voter Shifts in Australian Politics
item.description: The article explores the political landscape in Australia, focusing on the contrasting fortunes of two anti-establishment parties: Pauline Hanson's right-wing populism and the Greens. Since the federa...
